CONCLUSIONS
1. Under the Act a confllct of interest exists only rrhere an
official's 
rreconomic interestrt is involved in a decision. while
the circumstances in this case nay create the appearance of a

2 . Horrever, once Councilnernber I'Iolinari receives a

nore, he is r-quired to disqualify hinself for a period of 12

entity owned by David Perrin.

and

as
a result of-the nnewrt pending lawsuit, Councilmernber Molinari
coutd participate in decisions uhich lrou1d have a naterial
f inanc-lal effLct either on David Perrin or on a business entity in
which David Perrin has an ownership interest. 
Specifically, David
Perrin and Bucky Dennis (a dgfendant above) are partners in a
joint venture or partnership' t hich operates a concession, a
restaurant and banguet facility, 
at the city-owned golf course.
The decisions before the city council concern the proposal of
llessrs. Perrin and Dennis to expand the restaurant and banquet
facilities 
and to rnake other improvernents.
You were inforned on JuIy 9, 1991 that while it was true that
generally speaking lawsuits do not constitute an econonic interest
under seltlon azto:, monetary Judgments resulting therefrom do,
nanely as sources of incone to the counciLnenber.
ANALYSIS
Section 8710o of the Act prohibits any public official 
fron
rnaking, partlclpating in naking, or otherwise using.his-or her
official- positi6n to influence a governnental decision in which
the offlcial 
has a financial interest. 
section 87103 defines
Itf inancial interestrr as follous:
An official 
has a financial interest in a
decislon within the neaning of section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiIl
have a naterial financial effect, distinguishable
fron its effect on the public aenerally, on the

As a menber of the city council, t'!r. Holinari is a public
official under the Act. (Section 82048.) Consequently, he must
disgualify hinself fron any declsions which will have a
for6seeable nateriaL financial effect on his financial interests.
An effect is considered reasonably foreseeabl'e if there is a
substantial likelihood that it nilt occur. Certainty is not
required. However, if an effect is only a Dere possibility, it is
not reasonably foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops.
198, copy enclosed. )
The Lavrsuit
You and councilnan Molinari stated in telephone conversations
of Juty 9, 1991 that Councilnan ltlolinari is a party defendant in a
lawsuil in nhich David Perrin is the only plaintiff. 
David Perrin
$ras one of the four. naned defendants in a prior lawsuit. Perrin
v. Molinari is stilI in the early stages, and, as such, any
rnonetary award that the councilnenber nay receive from plaintiff
Perrin in the case j.s purely speculative at this tine. while the
circunstances present the apPearance of a conflict of interest, at
this point CounciLoenber trtolinari does not have any economic
interlst, as specified in Section 87103, in decisions concerning

-365
Page 5
the plaintiff as a result of the pending lawsuit. (Mullen Advice
Letter, No. I-91-194. )
For example, councj.Inenber uolinari wil.I not be directly
affected by the decisions. Decisions concerning the plaintiff's
request to expand the restaurant and banquet facilities and to
make improvenents will not result in the personal expenses,
incone,-assets, or liabilities of Councilmember Holinari or his
irnrnediate fanily increasing or decreasing by at Least $250.

has no investment interest or real. property involved in the
decisions. (Section 87103(a) and (b).) Further, such decisions
will not affect any person hlho has been a source of income or
gifts to the councilDenber, or who has pronised income or gifts to
€he councilEember, in the iast 12 nontha'5 (section 87103(c) and
(e) . ) Finally, the decision will not affect a business entity
with shich the councilnenber is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, enployee, or holds any position of nanagement.o (section
87103 (d) . )
Consequently, assuning 12 nonths have lapsed prior to the
time the decisions concerning !,!r. Perrin will cone before the city
council and since the date of receipt of the settlenent payment
fron l,tr. Perrin, the Act does not prohibit counciLnember Molinari
from participating in decisions concerning the plaintiff Perrin
under the current circunstances.

that a public officiit

whether the financial effect of a decision is naterial
depends on the speciflc circunstances of each decision. For
exinple, where a- party to the lawsuit is I'directly involved[ in a
deciiion before the city council, Regulation ].8702.1(a) provides

naterial and disqualification is required. (colobs Advice Letter,
No. A-89-177. ) C person or business entity is directly involved
in a decision before an officiaL's agency when that person or
entity, either personally or by an agent: (1) Initiates the
proceeding in which the decision witt be nade by filing an
application, claim, appeal, or sinilar request or; (2) Is a
nined party in, or is the subject of, the proceeding concerning
the decision before the official or the official's agency. A
person or business entity is the subject of a proceeding if a
decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or
revocation of any license, perroit, or other entitfenent to, or
contract with, the subject person or business entity. It viould
seem fron the telephone conversations that I have had with you and

that the city counciL decisions in question concern their
applications or proposals.

where any party to a larrsuit is not directly before the city

where a source of income is not a business entity and not

501(c) (5). )

section 82030 defines rrincomett as any payDent received,
unless expressly excluded from tbe deflnition in Section 82030(b).
rrPaynent[ is defined in section 82044 as any pa)rnent,
distribution, transfer, loan, advance, deposit, gift or other
renderinq of nonev, property, services or anything else of value,
whether tangible or intangible. Because roonetary judgments are
not specifiially excluded fron the definition of rinconert provided
in the Act, a nonetary jud$nent is considered income.
Conseguently, uhere the councilnenber receives a judgnent
fron any pirty to a lawsuit of $250 or nore, he has an econonic
interest in that party under Sectlon 87103. Sinilarly, he would
have an econonic inteiest in any party to a lawsuit if the suit
lrere settled and he received 92so or nore from that plaintiff or
defendant. The fact that pa)ment is rnade by an insurance carrier
on behalf of a party to a lawsuit does not alter this result. In
that circumstance the insurance cotlpany is merely acting as an
agent of the party to the lawsuit. It is the party or parties to
the lawsuit rLo are individually and jointly liable to
^^,.rncilmenber HoIinari. consequently, if the decisions before the
city council would foreseeably and naterially affect any party to
a tlwsuit who enters lnto a settlenent agreement with
-ouncilnenber Holinarl, or ls ordered by the court to pay hin a

co-urt-awarded Judguent fron each of those parties to the lawsuit.
(Jones Advice Letter, No. I-89-611.)
whether the financial effect of a decision is naterial
depends on the speciflc circunstances of each decision. For
exinple, where a- party to the lawsuit is I'directly involved[ in a
deciiion before the city council, Regulation ].8702.1(a) provides
that the effect of the decision on the source of incoroe is deemed
naterial and disqualification is required. (colobs Advice Letter,
No. A-89-177. ) C person or business entity is directly involved
in a decision before an officiaL's agency when that person or
entity, either personally or by an agent: (1) Initiates the
proceeding in which the decision witt be nade by filing an
application, claim, appeal, or sinilar request or; (2) Is a
nined party in, or is the subject of, the proceeding concerning
the decision before the official or the official's agency. A
person or business entity is the subject of a proceeding if a
decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or
revocation of any license, perroit, or other entitfenent to, or
contract with, the subject person or business entity. It viould
seem fron the telephone conversations that I have had with you and
Councilruenber Molinari that David Perrin and his business partner
Bucky Dennis (and naybe Phillip Pace) are vendors of the city and
that the city counciL decisions in question concern their
applications or proposals.

where any party to a larrsuit is not directly before the city
council, but nay be indirectly affected, Regnrlatl.ons 18702.2 and
]-a7o2.5 nay appLy. If the vendor's business enterprise is
operated for profit, Regnrlation ]-a7o2.2 provides that the
nateriality of the effect of a decision depends on the financial
size of the business entity. we refer you to that regulation for

vendor or vendors in question.
where a source of income is not a business entity and not
directly before the city council, Regulation 1a702.6 provides:
The effect of a decislon is naterial as to an
individual who is a source of incoue or gifts to an
official if any of the following applies:

(other than real
property) by 9r,000 or Eore; or

real property interest in a roanner that is
